title: castilleja douglasii subsp. insularis
intro: Castilleja douglasii subsp. insularis, also known as Island Indian Paintbrush, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant that is native to the Channel Islands in California. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Castilleja douglasii subsp. insularis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, although it's essential to consult with a medical professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
